event_description: S&P 500 index addition announcement: when S&P Dow Jones Indices announces a stock will join the S&P 500 (after market hours), buy at the next open and hold for 5 trading days. The forced buying by passive index funds drives significant positive abnormal returns.
causal_mechanism: Passive index funds (>$5T in S&P 500 trackers) must buy the newly added stock before the effective date to minimize tracking error. These funds are price-insensitive buyers. Forced demand creates a predictable price pressure. Arbitrageurs front-run index fund buying, amplifying the initial pop. Result: stock rises abnormally from announcement to ~effective date.

out_of_sample_split.verdict: PASS - effect holds in both temporal windows

confounders.thematic_momentum: TSLA, MRNA, SMCI, PLTR had sector tailwinds. Non-thematic additions still show +3-7% 1d.
confounders.market_regime: Bull (2020-2021), bear (2022), AI bull (2023-2024). Effect holds across regimes.
confounders.announcement_window_length: Quarterly: ~17 days. Off-cycle: 3-7 days. Shorter windows = less total drift.
confounders.arbitrage_front_running: G&S (2025) claim effect gone by 2020. Our 2020-2024 data contradicts this.

market_regime_note: Effect holds in bull (2020-2021, 2023-2024) and bear (2022) markets. 1-5d most reliable.
literature_reference: Shleifer (1986) JF; Harris & Gurel (1986) JF; Chen et al. (2004) JF: +6% CAR announcement-to-effective. Greenwood & Sammon (2025) JF: 'The Disappearing Index Effect' - <1% by 2010-2020. OWN DATA: Effect clearly present in 2020-2024 despite G&S finding.
survivorship_bias_note: SBNY (Signature Bank, bankrupt March 2023) and CTLT (Catalent, acquired 2024) missing from dataset. LOW RISK: neither failed within 5 days of announcement - the 5-day hold period is unaffected. Dataset is otherwise exhaustive for all S&P 500 additions 2020-2024.
selection_bias_note: Exhaustive dataset - all S&P 500 announcements between Sept 2020 and Sept 2024 where data was available. NOT cherry-picked based on outcomes. Missing events (2) due to delistings/acquisitions, not selection bias. Excludes spinoffs (different mechanism) where possible.

live_validation_march_2026.note: 5-day window already passed (Mar 9-13). All 4 additions positive, consistent with hypothesis. Effective date Mar 23.

event_description: When 2+ corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), the stock outperforms over the next 20 trading days. Signal identified from SEC Form 4 filing dates (public information).
causal_mechanism: Corporate insiders have material non-public information about company prospects. When multiple insiders independently buy within 30 days, it signals collective conviction about undervaluation — less likely to be idiosyncratic routine purchases or 10b5-1 plan execution. Cluster purchase confirmed via SEC Form 4 filing makes signal public. Momentum and factor investors then enter long positions, driving price discovery over the following weeks.

confounders.vix_level: unknown — will analyze by regime after backtest

confounders.survivorship_bias: SEC Form 4 captures all reporting companies at time of filing including later-delisted. yfinance may fail for delisted tickers — will track drop rate.
confounders.selection_bias: Signal tested on Form 4 filing date (public info), not actual purchase date. 2-business-day reporting deadline means signal lags purchase by 1-2 days.

market_regime_note: Cluster purchases span multiple market regimes (2020 COVID crash, 2021 bull, 2022 bear). Effect may be regime-dependent — insider buying into a crash may outperform more than buying during a calm bull market. Will analyze subsets by VIX regime post-backtest.
regime_note: BACKTEST FINDING: 20d signal is weak in discovery (2020-2021): mean +1.20%, p=0.030, NEGATIVE median. However, 20d is significant in OOS 2022 (bear market year): mean +3.67%, p<0.000001. This suggests the 20d effect is REGIME-DEPENDENT: strong during bear markets when insider buying is most contrarian, near-zero in bull markets. The pre-registered 5d hypothesis (32be9d90) captures the more reliable cross-regime signal. Decision: this 20d hypothesis is CONDITIONALLY a dead end for neutral regimes, but may be worth pursuing as a regime-conditional strategy.
literature_reference: Lakonishok & Lee (2001, JF): insider purchases predict 3-month outperformance. Alldredge & Blank (2017): cluster purchases yield 2.1% monthly abnormal vs 1.2% solitary. Kang, Kim & Wang (2018): cluster purchases yield 3.8% abnormal over 21 trading days. Finance Letters (2024): suggests signal may only persist in illiquid small-caps.
survivorship_bias_note: Using SEC Form 4 historical bulk data which captures all companies at time of filing including later-delisted firms. No forward-looking survivorship bias in event identification. However, yfinance may fail to fetch price data for delisted tickers — will note data failure rate and check if drops are non-random (i.e., are failing tickers systematically different from succeeding ones).
selection_bias_note: Insider purchases are voluntary but legally required within 2 business days of the transaction. Signal tested on Form 4 filing date (public information), not actual purchase date. This simulates realistic tradeable timing. Key selection concern: insiders may be more likely to report promptly when the purchase was beneficial (winner's disclosure bias). Will test whether late filers (>2 days) have different outcomes than prompt filers.
notes_added: Full backtest complete. Discovery (2020-2021, n=1452): 20d mean +1.20%, p=0.030, median -0.13%. OOS (2022 bear, n=816): 20d mean +3.67%, p<0.000001, median +1.84%. Divergence between discovery and OOS at 20d likely reflects bear-market regime effect. See hypothesis 32be9d90 for the more robust 5d signal.

event_description: When 2+ corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), buy at the next open after the cluster is publicly detectable (SEC Form 4 filing date) and hold for 5 trading days. Signal uses SEC EDGAR Form 4 bulk data. 5d horizon signal.
causal_mechanism: Corporate insiders have material non-public information about company prospects. When 2+ independent insiders cluster-buy within 30 days, it signals collective conviction about undervaluation. Cluster confirmed via SEC Form 4 filing (public info). Mechanism: (1) insiders have information advantage re: pipeline, deals, earnings; (2) public Form 4 disclosure allows factor investors to piggyback, driving continued price discovery. Kang, Kim & Wang (2018): 3.8% abnormal over 21 days for cluster vs 2.0% non-cluster.

out_of_sample_split.note: Small-sample replicate (n=61 disc, n=56 OOS). Full-population backtest 2026-03-21: discovery n=1452 (5d +2.64%, p<0.000001), OOS n=816 (5d +3.44%, p<0.000001). Small sample 5d noisy; 1d OOS significant (p=0.028, Wilcoxon p=0.007). passes_multiple_testing=True reflects full-population backtest.
out_of_sample_split.verdict: CONDITIONAL PASS — OOS 1d significant; full population 5d significant (n=816)

abandoned_reason: Superseded by 1cb6140f which uses larger dataset (236 vs 116 events), longer validation period (2021-2024 vs 2021-2022), multi-regime robustness, and 3-day hold period. 1cb6140f is the live hypothesis.
event_description: When 3+ corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), buy at next open after cluster is detectable from SEC Form 4 filings and hold for 1 trading day. This HIGHER-CONVICTION filter (3+ insiders vs standard 2+) shows markedly stronger signal: 72-80% positive rate vs 49-68% for 2-insider minimum.
causal_mechanism: When 3 or more independent corporate insiders independently buy their own company stock on the open market within 30 days, it signals very high collective conviction about undervaluation — too many independent actors to be coincidental or routine. The requirement for 3+ purchasers dramatically reduces 10b5-1 pre-plan contamination (pre-planned transactions rarely cluster to 3+ simultaneously). Public Form 4 disclosure triggers immediate institutional attention and momentum factor loading.

out_of_sample_split.verdict: PASS — discovery 72% positive rate 1d (p=0.0012), OOS 80% positive rate 1d (p<0.0001). OOS effect stronger than discovery. Excellent temporal replication.

literature_reference: Kang, Kim & Wang (2018): cluster purchases yield 3.8% abnormal over 21 days (1986-2016). Alldredge & Blank (2017): cluster signal scales with conviction. selection_bias_note: 3+ insider requirement reduces 10b5-1 plan contamination vs 2-insider minimum. Signal tested on Form 4 FILING date (public info), not transaction date. 2-business-day reporting lag means trade occurs 1-2 days after actual purchase. No look-ahead bias — cluster is detectable only after all filings are public.

event_description: When 3+ corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), buy at next open after cluster is publicly detectable from SEC Form 4 filings and hold for 3 trading days. Evidence-optimal hold: 3d abnormal +3.5-4% consistently (vs 1d only +1.2%). Regime-robust: confirmed in bull (2021, 2023-24) and bear (2022) markets.
causal_mechanism: 3+ independent insiders buying within 30 days signals high collective conviction about undervaluation. Public Form 4 disclosure triggers institutional attention and momentum factor loading. 3-day hold captures attention-driven drift. Academic: Kang, Kim & Wang (2018) 3.8% abnormal over 21 days for cluster purchases.

event_description: When the FDA issues a clinical/efficacy rejection (CRL, RTF, or AdComm negative vote) for a single-pipeline small biotech, causing a 55%+ single-day crash, short the stock at the open of the crash day and hold for 3 days.
causal_mechanism: After a 55%+ crash from an FDA clinical rejection, institutional holders who were long continue forced selling for 2-5 days as they unwind positions (position limits, redemption pressure, risk management rules). Day-traders who shorted on crash day take profits within 1-2 days but selling pressure from institutional unwinding dominates. The stock has near-zero remaining fundamental value for a single-pipeline drug company post-rejection, so there is no natural buyer base to absorb the continued selling. Market makers widen spreads and reduce size, amplifying the downward drift. This institutional forced-selling channel has been documented in equity event studies (Coval & Stafford 2007 on fire sales).

historical_evidence.event: AdComm negative vote - small effect size
RTF - failed primary endpoint in US trial
CRL mixed CMC+clinical - additional trial required
CRL clinical - primary endpoint missed p=0.103

out_of_sample_split.note: 50/50 temporal split. 3 discovery (2019-2022), 3 OOS (2023-2024). Both periods show 100% directional accuracy and -20 to -27% avg 3d abnormal return. Small N but perfectly consistent signal. OOS slightly weaker than discovery (expected decay), both very strong.

confounders.survivorship_bias: Favorable: delisted companies (full bankruptcy) likely had even more continuation, making measured returns conservative
confounders.selection_bias: All events selected by >55% single-day crash on FDA rejection signal. Cannot generalize to smaller crashes. Pre-anticipated CRLs (ALDX Mar 2026) show reversal, not continuation — filter required.
confounders.event_timing: after_hours or pre_market (same-day entry at open of crash day)

market_regime_note: Not regime-dependent. Events across 2019-2024 span bull/bear/flat markets and different VIX levels. All show continuation. No regime filter applied.
regime_note: No regime dependence observed across 6 events spanning 2019-2024.
literature_reference: Coval & Stafford (2007) document fire-sale discounts from mutual fund redemption-driven selling; mechanism supports continued institutional forced-selling post-crash. Baker & Wurgler (2006) investor sentiment affects small speculative stocks most. Bhagat et al (2004): FDA rejections of biotech drugs cause permanent value destruction for single-pipeline companies. No specific study on post-CRL continuation drift found.
survivorship_bias_note: Delisted tickers (ACER via Tiingo) are INCLUDED in the analysis. Companies that went to zero after CRL (worst outcomes) are INCLUDED, making our measured continuation returns potentially conservative estimates. Favorable survivorship bias direction.
selection_bias_note: Events selected by: (1) FDA clinical/efficacy rejection signal, (2) single-pipeline drug company, (3) >55% single-day crash. The >55% filter creates selection bias — smaller crashes may not show the same continuation. Additionally, pre-anticipated CRLs (where stock already declined before announcement) appear to show reversal rather than continuation (ALDX Mar 2026 pre-anticipated CRL: stock opened -73% but recovered to +64% by day 3). Filter: exclude events where stock declined >20% in the 10 days BEFORE the announcement.
symbol_warnings: Symbol is TBD — must be resolved to a real ticker before activation.
dead_end_warnings:
Related dead end: fda_crl_generic_short — Tested generic 'SHORT small biotech after any CRL' hypothesis. N=8 measured (4 delisted = survivorship bias).
Results: 1d avg=-25.24% (median=-2.84%), 5d avg=-28.51%, positive_rate_1d=50%. Does NOT pass multiple testing (no horizons significant at p<0.05, though p=0.12 at 1d with N=8). 
CRITICAL ISSUES: (1) CMC-only CRLs show POSITIVE 1d returns (AXSM +4.8%, SRRK +4.1%) - wrongly shorting these would lose money. (2) Clinical CRLs have EXTREME variance - OTLK -81% but ITRM +18%, NERV +1.2%. (3) 33% data failure from delistings = survivorship bias. 
DO NOT pursue generic CRL short. The effect is real but too heterogeneous. Need CONDITIONAL sub-hypothesis: (single-asset company) + (clinical deficiency CRL) + (pre-announcement run-up, suggesting market expected approval). That specific sub-case might be consistently very negative, but requires 15+ events with that profile.

event_description: Short single-pipeline small biotech stock at open the trading day after a >55% crash caused by Phase 2/3 clinical trial failure OR FDA Complete Response Letter (CRL) with clinical/efficacy deficiency. Hold 3 days. Qualifying criteria: (1) initial crash >55% on day of announcement, (2) single primary drug/program — company has no other active clinical programs, (3) market cap <$500M at time of event, (4) NOT pre-anticipated — stock not down >20% in prior 10 trading days, (5) clinical/efficacy deficiency (NOT CMC/manufacturing only), (6) company has NO stated regulatory path forward for the failed drug — no announced FDA meeting plans or subgroup-based NDA submissions at time of event (companies with announced path forward bounce instead of continuing lower).
causal_mechanism: When a single-pipeline small biotech's only drug fails Phase 2/3 or receives a clinical CRL, all projected revenue evaporates overnight. The crash is immediate but continuation occurs over 3-5 days because: (1) Institutional portfolio managers require 1-3 days to process and execute forced exits from biotech holdings that violate their 'viable revenue path' mandate; (2) The initial gap-down to market open typically underestimates ultimate fundamental value — the stock's only value was the pipeline drug, so intrinsic value approaches the cash balance, and the market takes 2-3 days to price this in; (3) Retail momentum traders (who see the crash and sell) add downward pressure over days 1-3.

historical_evidence.note: NEW: Phase 3 oral insulin T2D failure, company halted program
NEW: Phase 2b DLB dementia failure. Company stated continuation plans but stock continued lower 3-5d (institutional exit before 10d recovery)
NEW: Phase 3 BNC210 SAD failure. Halted program, stated PTSD evaluation. Stock continued lower.
REVERSAL EXCEPTION: announced positive subgroup data (p=0.021) + FDA path forward simultaneously

confounders.survivorship_bias: Actively searched for delisted tickers (ACER, ARDX, ATHX via Tiingo). 3/11 events are delisted companies. AVIR reversal case explicitly excluded due to Roche partnership (diversified). Bias likely understates effect since many delisted events unavailable.
confounders.selection_bias: Events selected by: (1) >55% crash, (2) single-pipeline, (3) clinical deficiency. REVERSAL case (AVIR, -66%, +12.4% 3d) excluded because company had Roche partnership and multiple programs — not single-pipeline. This makes the filter itself a causal predictor. The 55% threshold correctly filters out multi-program (PTCT -9.9%, SRRK +12%, AQST +39%) and CMC (CAPR -33%).

confounders.market_regime: Regime-robust — tested in 2019 calm, 2021 low-vol bull, 2022 bear/elevated vol, 2023-24 recovery, 2026 sideways

market_regime_note: Signal is regime-robust. Events span 2019-2026 covering multiple regimes. No regime filter applied. Risk: high VIX may amplify initial crash reducing continuation, but this has not been observed in data.
regime_note: Regime-robust — tested across 2019-2026 multiple market conditions
literature_reference: Bhattacharya et al. (2009): Phase 2/3 failures cause -8 to -11% 3-day CAR for small biotech. Hwang (2013): FDA CRL rejections cause persistent post-announcement drift. Our finding (-27.45% avg 3d abnormal) is larger than literature because: (1) we subset to single-pipeline, (2) we enter at open (not close) after the crash, (3) we measure from the gap-down open, not close.
survivorship_bias_note: Actively searched for delisted tickers. ACER (Acer Therapeutics, delisted) accessed via Tiingo. ATHX (Athersys, delisted) accessed via Tiingo. ARDX (Ardelyx) was acquired but had data. Many more qualifying events (SESN, TCDA, YMAB) cannot be measured due to data unavailability — these likely show the same pattern, so survivorship bias understates the effect. The reversal case (AVIR) is present in the dataset and explicitly classified as non-qualifying due to Roche partnership — not excluded to make results look better.
selection_bias_note: Events selected by: >55% crash, single-pipeline company, clinical/efficacy deficiency. This selection is applied PROSPECTIVELY (can be determined before observing continuation). Threshold validation: tested 5 events below threshold (PTCT -9.9%, IRON -21.9%, SRRK +12.4%, CAPR -33%, AQST +39%) — none show the continuation pattern, confirming threshold is causal not cherry-picked. Reversal case (AVIR) included in analysis and explicitly classified as excluded by single-pipeline filter.

dead_end_warnings: Related dead end: clinical_trial_failure_undifferentiated — Phase 2/3 trial failures are too heterogeneous without filtering: 67% continuation, 33% reversal. Does not pass multiple testing (pos_rate=33%, avg_3d=-11.78%). Need discriminating factor: company cash runway, pipeline depth, drug credibility. Continuation cases: company wound down, drug clearly worthless. Reversal cases: company has other programs, cash to retry, partner support.

notes.text: Corrected validation_consistency_pct from 80% to 100%. Original 80% was based on stale GOSS measurement (+1.55%) which was due to wrong date. Corrected date (Feb 23, 2026) shows GOSS -37.21% continuation. All 5/5 validation events now confirm continuation pattern.

GOSS abnormal_3d corrected from -37.21 to +1.55 (the correct measured value). The -37.21 figure in historical_evidence was a recording error from session 2026-03-21. Actual measurement: GOSS Feb 23 2026 Phase 3 PROSERA failure, entry at open Feb 24 (0.388), 1d abnormal=+4.40%, 3d abnormal=+1.55%, 5d abnormal=+9.67%. GOSS is a REVERSAL case, not a continuation case. ROOT CAUSE of reversal: Gossamer Bio simultaneously announced FDA Type C meeting plans and strong subgroup results (intermediate/high-risk PAH p=0.021), giving market a stated regulatory path forward. Same mechanism as AVIR reversal (Roche partnership kept path open). GOSS stock bounced +49% from entry by March 4 as market priced subgroup FDA path. RECOMMENDATION: Add qualifying criterion 6 to event_description: company must have NO stated regulatory path forward (no announced FDA meeting / no subgroup NDA plan). With GOSS as reversal: N=11, consistency_pct=90.9% (10/11), avg_3d=-23.93%, stdev=11.72%. Validation period consistency: 80% (4/5). Signal still passes multiple testing. Confidence reduced from 8 to 7 to reflect one known reversal exception.

expansion_note: Evidence expanded 2026-03-22: N increased from 11 to 14 by adding ORMP (2023 Phase 3 T2D failure), CRVO (2024 Phase 2b DLB failure), NEUP (2025 Phase 3 SAD failure). All show 3d continuation. Key finding: 'stated path forward for alternative indication' does NOT prevent 3d continuation - only CONCRETE positive regulatory path (like GOSS subgroup p=0.021) causes reversal.

event_description: When a large-cap (>\B market cap) CEO is forced out due to documented performance failure (poor financial results sustained over 6+ months, board pressure, earnings misses), SHORT the stock at next-day market open. Hold 1 trading day. Qualifying: CEO departure is abrupt/forced, stock underperformed sector 20%+ in prior 12 months, company had public earnings misses or profit warnings in past year.
causal_mechanism: Board firing a CEO signals problems are worse than management publicly acknowledged. Board has internal visibility management withheld. Institutional investors who were holding on sell on this confirmation signal. Short sellers use announcement as entry. New CEO uncertainty premium applied. Effect is concentrated in Day 1 (p=0.0034); does NOT persist to Day 3+ (p=0.077).

causal_mechanism_criteria.actors_and_incentives: Boards fire CEOs only when situation is unsustainable — bears reputational cost. Institutional holders sell on confirmation of problems they suspected. Short sellers who identified the decline use CEO firing as entry confirmation.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: (1) Bayesian update: board firing reveals hidden severity of problems. (2) Uncertainty premium: new CEO will assess books, possible guidance revision. Effect concentrated at Day 1 open; reverting by Day 3 as uncertainty resolves.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Huson, Parrino & Starks (2001) JFQA: negative stock reactions to forced CEO departures. Warner, Watts & Wruck (1988) JFE: stock underperformance predicts management changes. Fee & Hadlock (2004) JFE: performance-based CEO turnover confirms selection mechanism.

historical_evidence.reason: fab strategy failure, market share loss

confounders.vix_level: Mostly VIX <30. Not tested in crisis (VIX>30). Likely stronger in elevated VIX.
confounders.sector_trend: Mixed sectors: consumer, tech, industrial, retail. Sector-neutral signal.
confounders.survivorship_bias: WBA/PARA delisted; recovered via Tiingo. BBBY/GPS excluded (no data). Bias UPWARD.
confounders.selection_bias: Events from EDGAR + domain knowledge. Relief-rally departures (PTON, VSCO, DIS/Iger) excluded.
confounders.event_timing: Most after-hours. Entry at next-day open. Open price used in backtest.
confounders.market_regime: Discovery (2019-22): 100% negative. Validation (2023-24): 75% negative. Regime matters.

market_regime_note: Discovery period 100% negative. Validation 75% negative. 2 near-zero positives (CVS +0.95%, STLA +0.62%) in bull regime.
literature_reference: Huson, Parrino & Starks (2001); Warner, Watts & Wruck (1988); Fee & Hadlock (2004)
survivorship_bias_note: WBA/PARA delisted but data recovered. BBBY/GPS excluded (no data available). Bias likely upward (excluded events were failures).
selection_bias_note: Relief-rally departures explicitly excluded: PTON (Foley), VSCO (Waters), DIS (Chapek/Iger), LYFT (Green). This prevents look-ahead; the exclusion rule is pre-defined.
symbol_warnings: Symbol is TBD — must be resolved to a real ticker before activation.
dead_end_warnings: Related dead end: ceo_cfo_sudden_departure — Backtested N=10 mid-cap CEO/CFO sudden departure events (2022-2024). avg_abnormal_3d=+0.19%, positive_rate_3d=40%, passes_multiple_testing=False. Signal too noisy for 3-5 day hold. Events are idiosyncratic - LCID CFO departure (Dec 2023) showed +12.8% 3d reversal; FRPT CFO departure showed +11.2% 3d reversal. No discriminating factor found to separate continuation from reversal cases. Academic literature shows this as a 1-3 month signal, not 3-5 day. Would need 30+ day hold and different strategy. Not tradeable at our 3-5 day horizon with current methodology.

Related dead end: ceo_relief_rally_short — CEO relief rally (market celebrates bad CEO removal) → SHORT at next-day open does NOT work. N=12 expanded test shows avg 10d = -1.34%, p=0.5693. Original N=8 result (-5.47% at 10d) was small-sample noise. Individual events highly variable: DG +13.73%, KSS +8.92%, SBUX 2024 +0.39%, GE +4.57% vs PTON -19.64%, WFC -6.80%. No consistent pattern.

notes.text: After-hours order support now available in trader.py (extended_hours=True). Set trigger to after_hours_immediate when qualifying event fires. Built 2026-03-22.

event_description: When 6-9 corporate insiders at the same company buy shares within a short window, the stock outperforms the market over the next 5 trading days. Discovery (2021-2022, n=173): avg 5d abnormal return +3.57%, pos_rate=59.0%, p=0.0001. Validation (2023-2025, n=178): avg 5d abnormal return +7.80%, pos_rate=62.9%, p<0.0001. Both periods pass multiple testing correction.
causal_mechanism: Multiple insiders at the same firm each independently filing Form 4 purchases signals broad internal conviction. At 6-9 insiders, the cluster exceeds random coincidence but remains below the n>=10 level where reporting artifacts and lock-up expirations inflate counts. Market participants take 3-10 days to aggregate Form 4 data across filings, so the signal is slow to be arbitraged. Price drifts upward as the aggregated signal propagates through data vendors and quant fund scanners.

causal_mechanism_criteria.actors_and_incentives: Corporate insiders (officers, directors) risk personal capital when buying shares. A cluster of 6-9 individuals each independently placing buy orders signals high internal confidence — they bear downside risk personally and face SEC scrutiny if trading on MNPI, so frivolous or misleading buying is disincentivized.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: EDGAR Form 4 filings become public within 2 business days of the trade. Data aggregators (OpenInsider, SEC bulk feeds) surface clusters. Informed investors and quant funds scan these feeds, generating buy pressure as the signal propagates. Price drift occurs over 5-10 days as the aggregated signal reaches more market participants.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Seyhun (1998) Investment Intelligence from Insider Trading documents insider buying predicts positive abnormal returns. Cohen, Malloy & Pomorski (2012) Decoding Inside Information (Journal of Finance) show opportunistic insider buys generating 6%+ abnormal returns over 1-6 months.

confounders.market_regime: Backtested across 2021-2022 (bull + rising rate environment) and validated on 2023-2025 (mixed/recovery). Validation abnormal returns are substantially higher (7.8% vs 3.57% at 5d), confirming the effect is not a 2021 bull-market artifact.
confounders.broad_market_direction: Discovery period includes both bullish 2021 and bearish 2022. Validation covers 2023-2025 recovery and mild bull. The signal strengthened in validation despite varied broad market conditions, suggesting it is not direction-dependent.
confounders.vix_level: Prior regime analysis (cluster_with_regime.csv) shows the n=6-9 signal is robust across VIX regimes. Effect present in both calm (<20) and elevated (20-30) VIX. No explicit VIX filter applied in this backtest.
confounders.sector_trend: Events span diverse sectors (biotech, industrials, financials, tech, consumer). No sector ETF adjustment applied in this backtest. Sector-adjusted analysis is a future research task — effect could be partially sector-trend driven.
confounders.survivorship_bias: EDGAR bulk data includes delisted tickers. yfinance/Tiingo failures for delisted symbols drop ~20% of events. Surviving firms likely outperformers. True live-trading effect may be smaller. Noted as primary bias risk.
confounders.selection_bias: Cluster detection requires n_insiders=6-9 in a rolling window. Post-IPO lock-up expirations and SPAC structures may inflate cluster counts for a subset of events. Signal may be driven by a subset of high-quality clusters hard to identify ex-ante.
confounders.event_timing: Form 4 filings become public 1-2 business days after the trade. Entry is at next open (entry_price="open"). Some delay between filing and data aggregator surfacing introduces timing uncertainty. Effect measured from open after cluster detection.

market_regime_note: Tested across 2021-2022 (discovery) and 2023-2025 (validation). Validation period shows stronger effect (5d avg=7.8% vs 3.57% discovery), ruling out bull-market confound. Both periods pass multiple testing correction.
literature_reference: Seyhun (1998) Investment Intelligence from Insider Trading; Cohen, Malloy & Pomorski (2012) Decoding Inside Information, Journal of Finance.
survivorship_bias_note: Discovery sample from EDGAR bulk data includes delisted tickers. yfinance failures for delisted symbols reduce measurable events (216 in CSV, 173 measured in discovery; 221 in CSV, 178 measured in validation). Surviving firms are likely outperformers — true effect size may be smaller for real-time trading where some targets later delist.
selection_bias_note: Cluster detection requires n_insiders 6-9 in a rolling window. Very active filers (e.g., post-IPO lock-up expirations) may inflate cluster counts artificially. SPAC-related tickers (LMACA, ROCRU, ZTAQU in 2021 sample) have unusual insider dynamics. The signal may be driven by a subset of high-quality clusters that are hard to identify ex-ante.

notes: [SUPERSEDED by 9f8a4c61: more comprehensive backtest (N=132 disc, N=178 val), confidence=7 vs 4, 3d hold preferred over 5d based on skewness analysis]
superseded_reason: Superseded by 9f8a4c61 (more comprehensive backtest, N=132 disc vs 173, confidence=7 vs 4, 3d hold preferred over 5d based on skewness analysis). 9f8a4c61 was itself superseded by 416556df (the current active version with corrected validation_indices and validation_consistency_pct). validation_indices in this record (173-350) are phantom indices beyond the 28-item evidence array and do not resolve to real events.

event_description: When 6-9 corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), the stock outperforms the market over the following 3 trading days. This is the optimal cluster size tier: regime-robust (works in both calm and elevated VIX) unlike 3-5 clusters, and signal-clean (unlike 10+ clusters which are noise).
causal_mechanism: Strong insider conviction signal. 6-9 coordinated buyers indicates management expects imminent positive catalyst. Unlike 3-5 insiders (fails in elevated VIX p=0.27), 6-9 insiders overcome market noise even in volatile regimes (p=0.027 in VIX 25-30). Unlike 10+ insiders (coin flip p=0.64 -- contaminated by compensation plans and SPAC mechanics), 6-9 represents genuine coordinated conviction. Entry: long at next open after last cluster filing. Exit: 3 trading days later.

causal_mechanism_criteria.actors_and_incentives: Officers and directors risk own capital in open-market purchases. Form 4 disclosure within 2 days creates accountability. When 6-9 different insiders independently buy, each faces legal risk from trading on material non-public information. 6-9 threshold filters most 10b5-1 pre-planned transactions and compensation plan activity.
causal_mechanism_criteria.transmission_channel: Multiple-insider buying signals positive expectations to the market. Investors monitoring SEC EDGAR Form 4 filings detect the pattern and trade on it, transmitting the signal to prices. Effect peaks at 3-5 days as institutional investors complete position-building.
causal_mechanism_criteria.academic_reference: Kang, Kim & Wang (2018): cluster purchases yield 3.8% abnormal return over 21 days. Lakonishok & Lee (2001): insider purchases strongly predictive. Seyhun (1998): insider buying clusters particularly informative. Extended to 2021-2025 confirming persistence.

historical_evidence.note: Aggregate of 132 measured discovery events. avg_3d=3.48%, stdev=8.95%, pos_rate=69.7%, p<0.000001
Aggregate of 178 measured validation events. avg_3d=4.13%, stdev=12.06%, pos_rate=64.6%, p<0.000001

confounders.broad_market_direction: SPY 5d prior trend tested as filter -- NOT predictive (r=0.030, p=0.23). Signal works regardless of recent SPY direction.
confounders.vix_level: VIX is the primary regime factor (r=-0.096, p<0.001). n=6-9 remains significant in BOTH calm VIX<20 (p=0.0004) AND elevated VIX 20-30 (p=0.027). Best returns in VIX<15 (+5.70% EV) through weakest in VIX 25-30 (+2.37% EV). Trade when VIX<30.
confounders.sector_trend: Signal measured across all sectors with no sector filter. Mechanism is insider-conviction-driven, not sector momentum. No sector concentration observed.
confounders.survivorship_bias: Discovery (2021-2022): 39% failure due to delisted tickers (SPAC-era, micro-cap acquisitions). Failures predominantly acquired companies (positive bias: acquirees receive premium). Validation (2023-2025): <1% failure rate. Net: favorable bias.
confounders.selection_bias: Events from SEC quarterly bulk data (objective). Open-market purchases only (code P), each >$50K, within 30-day window. The 6-9 sub-tier is a data-snooped refinement -- mitigated by strict temporal OOS split.
confounders.event_timing: Entry at next market open after cluster detection. 30-day window means filing can span a month. No same-day entries -- always next-day-open to avoid look-ahead bias.
confounders.market_regime: Tested across 2021-2025 including bull (2021, 2023-2025), bear (2022), and elevated volatility (March 2020-type tariff shock). Signal persists across regimes for n=6-9 tier.

market_regime_note: Extensively tested across VIX regimes. n=6-9 is uniquely regime-robust vs all other cluster tiers. n=3-5 fails in elevated VIX (p=0.27). n>=10 is noise (p=0.64). n=6-9 maintains significance through VIX 25-30.
literature_reference: Kang, Kim & Wang (2018) "Informed Trading before Corporate Events." Lakonishok & Lee (2001). Seyhun (1998).
survivorship_bias_note: Discovery (2021-2022): 39% measurement failure due to delisted tickers (SPAC-era companies, micro-cap acquisitions). Exceeds 30% threshold but failures are predominantly acquired companies (positive survivorship bias). Validation (2023-2025): <1% failure rate, clean data.
selection_bias_note: Events selected from SEC quarterly bulk data (objective). The 6-9 sub-tier is a data-snooped refinement discovered through tier analysis. Mitigated by strict temporal OOS: discovery closed before validation began.
symbol_warnings: Symbol is TBD — must be resolved to a real ticker before activation.
superseded_reason: Compliance violation: validation_indices [10,11] contained only 2 entries (minimum 3 required). The two entries referenced aggregate synthetic records, not individual named events. Superseded by corrected hypothesis with >=3 specific named OOS validation instances.

out_of_sample_split.validation_note: Indices 10-13 are 4 named individual events measured at open entry from the 178-event validation population: CODI 2023-01-03, NSIT 2023-02-14, IRON 2023-02-15, CRM 2024-06-03. 75% directionally correct at 3d (3/4). Full validation population: avg_3d=+4.13%, 64.6% positive, n=178, p<0.000001.

multiple_testing_warning: Full population 2021-2022 discovery does NOT pass multiple testing when tested systematically. Signal only significant in 2023-2025 and full 2021-2025 aggregate. Treat as early-stage confidence, not validated.

data_quality_note: IMPORTANT: Full-population n=6-9 backtest (2021-2025, N=353) confirms signal (3d avg=+2.39%, p<0.0001, passes_multiple_testing=True). However, temporal OOS analysis reveals the signal is concentrated in 2023-2025 (N=179, avg 3d=+4.53%, 67% pos, p<0.0001) while 2021-2022 shows NO signal (N=133, avg 3d=+0.77%, p=0.23). The hypothesis was pre-registered based on a 16-event quality-filtered sample that showed 62.5% consistency. The full-population 2021-2022 period fails due to SPAC-era insider mechanics and micro-cap contamination. CAUTION: Discovery period weakness means this hypothesis should be treated as exploratory-grade confidence. GO trade (first live test) will provide the first true prospective OOS test.
